F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When the protagonist arrives at someone’s home, the protagonist greets the head of the household first and then greets the other guests.

GPT-5.2.name: Tomás Andrade
GPT-5.2.story:
Tomás climbed the stairs to a second-floor apartment in Viña del Mar carrying a bottle of carménère in one hand. He heard voices inside before the door opened. A woman in an apron smiled and said, “Hello, come in.”

Tomás looked directly at her and stepped forward first. “Good evening, Mrs. Patricia. Thank you very much for inviting us,” he said, holding the bottle out with both hands. She took it and replied, “How kind.”

Only after that did Tomás turn toward the living room where people were gathered around a coffee table with pebre and bread. He moved along the circle, greeting one by one. “Hello, Felipe,” he said, offering a handshake. “Hello, Antonia,” he said, leaning in for a quick cheek kiss. A man near the window raised a glass, and Tomás walked over and said, “Nice to meet you,” before taking the offered hand.

Patricia pointed toward a tray of empanadas. Tomás waited until the person beside him stepped aside, then walked back through the room and said, “Where should I leave this?” He placed his jacket on the hook by the door when she indicated the hallway, and then returned to the group.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the protagonist is unsure who the head of the household is, the protagonist greets everyone respectfully and asks discreetly who is hosting before offering a special greeting.

GPT-5.2.name: Paula Espinoza
GPT-5.2.story:
Paula arrived at a birthday gathering in Antofagasta with a small box of alfajores. The door was open, and two people stood in the entryway talking. Paula smiled and greeted both. “Hello, good afternoon,” she said, offering a quick cheek kiss to each.

Inside, she saw several relatives moving between the dining table and the kitchen. Paula greeted them as she passed: “Hello, how are you?” and “Nice to meet you.” A man carrying plates said, “Come right in,” and she stepped aside to let him through.

Paula paused near the hallway where the noise was lower and leaned toward a woman arranging cups. “Excuse me,” Paula said softly, “who is receiving guests today? I do not want to make a mistake.” The woman nodded toward the kitchen. “My mother, Mrs. Marta, is the one in charge.”

Paula walked to the kitchen doorway and waited until Marta finished rinsing a bowl. Then Paula stepped forward and said, “Mrs. Marta, thank you very much for receiving us,” holding out the alfajores. Marta wiped her hands on a towel, took the box, and said, “How lovely, thank you.” Paula stayed nearby while Marta set the box on the counter.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist greets the head of the household first, but then treats other family members as less important and talks over them.

GPT-5.2.name: Rodrigo Paredes
GPT-5.2.story:
Rodrigo arrived at a family lunch in Talca with a bag of pastries. The front door opened, and an older man said, “Hello, I am Don Ernesto.” Rodrigo smiled and reached out immediately. “Don Ernesto, nice to greet you,” he said, shaking his hand firmly and handing over the bag.

Don Ernesto led him into the living room where several family members were gathered. A teenage girl started to introduce herself. “Hello, I am—” Rodrigo cut in, talking over her. “Yes, yes, hello,” he said, already turning toward an uncle who was pouring drinks.

A woman offered him a seat. Rodrigo answered without looking at her, “I’ll sit wherever,” and then began speaking loudly to don Ernesto across the room. “Don Ernesto, you should really try that new restaurant on Avenida Colon,” he said, continuing even as two people tried to add comments.

At the table, a younger cousin asked, “Would you like salad?” Rodrigo kept his hands on the chair back and spoke past him to don Ernesto. “So, about the football match last week,” he said, and the cousin held the salad bowl in midair for a second before setting it down. Don Ernesto cleared his throat and motioned everyone toward their seats while Rodrigo kept talking.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When the protagonist arrives at someone’s home, the protagonist ignores the head of the household and greets others first as if the host does not matter.

GPT-5.2.name: Nina Carter
GPT-5.2.story:
Nina arrived at a house in Puerto Varas with a small gift bag and rang the bell. The door opened, and a woman said, “Hello, I am Daniela.” Nina stepped past the threshold with a quick “Hi,” barely looking at her.

She spotted two guests she had met earlier at a tour and headed straight toward them in the living room. “Oh my god, you guys!” she said, hugging them. She turned her back to the entrance while Daniela stood by the door holding it open.

Nina pointed at the snacks and said loudly, “I’m starving,” then asked one of the guests, “Where do they keep the drinks?” Without waiting, she walked into the kitchen, opened a cabinet, and pulled out a glass. Daniela followed and said, “If you want, I can show you,” but Nina kept talking to the guests over her shoulder.

A man approached from the hallway and said, “Welcome.” Nina waved and said, “Hey,” then returned to her conversation. Daniela placed a bowl of sopaipillas on the table and said, “Serve yourselves,” while Nina sat down between the guests she knew, still not having spoken directly to the person who opened the door.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When the protagonist is invited for once, the protagonist recognizes it as a distinct late-afternoon or evening tea-style meal and participates appropriately with conversation and light eating.

GPT-5.2.name: Antonia Fuentes
GPT-5.2.story:
Antonia arrived at her neighbor’s apartment in Temuco at seven, carrying a small bag of pan amasado from the bakery on the corner. The message had said, “Come for once,” and Antonia had eaten only a light snack earlier.

Inside, the table was set with mugs, a teapot, and plates of palta, queso fresco, and jam. “Come in, Anto,” said Patricia, the host. “Sit down, we are just getting started.”

Antonia put the bread on the table. “I brought warm bread,” she said, unwrapping it. She poured herself tea when Patricia offered. “Black tea or herbal tea?”

“Herbal, please,” Antonia replied.

They spread palta on marraquetas and ate small bites while talking. Antonia asked, “How is your son doing with school?” and listened as Patricia explained a new schedule. When another neighbor mentioned the bus strike last week, Antonia chimed in. “I had to walk all the way to Avenida Alemania,” she said, shaking her head and laughing once.

She didn’t look for a main dish or ask when dinner would be. Instead, she took a second small piece of bread, added a thin slice of cheese, and sipped tea between sentences.

After a while, Patricia brought out a plate of kuchen. Antonia accepted a small slice and said, “Just a small piece,” then leaned back to keep chatting while the kettle steamed softly.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the protagonist has dietary limits, the protagonist joins for tea or coffee, eats what is suitable, and stays engaged socially.

GPT-5.2.name: Lorena Saavedra
GPT-5.2.story:
Lorena arrived at her friend Paula’s home in Chillán just after sunset. Paula had texted, “Once at my house,” and Lorena brought a small box of tea bags she liked. The table held bread, butter, avocado, and a plate of sliced kuchen.

“Come in, Lore,” Paula said. “Coffee or tea?”

“Tea, thank you,” Lorena replied. “And I’ll tell you right away: I can’t eat flour today, but I’m still happy to come.”

Paula nodded. “Do not worry. I also have fruit.” She brought a bowl of grapes and a couple of mandarins.

Lorena took tea and peeled a mandarin, placing the peel neatly on the edge of her plate. “How is your mother doing?” she asked, leaning forward.

Paula answered, and Lorena listened, making small sounds of acknowledgement and asking one or two follow-up questions. When the conversation shifted to work, Lorena added, “At my job they changed the shift this week,” then sipped tea.

Paula offered bread again. “Are you sure you do not want even a small piece?”

“Really, better not,” Lorena said, smiling. “But thanks.” She took a few grapes instead and kept talking with the others.

When someone mentioned a new bakery nearby, Lorena asked where it was and wrote the street name in her phone. She stayed at the table, cup in hand, while Paula poured more hot water into the teapot.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist attends once, but makes dismissive jokes about the custom that make the host feel ridiculed.

GPT-5.2.name: Benjamín Silva
GPT-5.2.story:
Benjamín arrived at his girlfriend’s family house in Talcahuano at the time they told him, and her mother said, “All right, sit down; we are having once.” The table was set with tea, bread, and small plates of queso and mermelada.

Benjamín sat down and looked around. “Tea-time meal? So... is this like a pretend dinner?” he said, chuckling.

His girlfriend, Javiera, nudged him under the table. “Don’t say that.”

Her mother poured him tea. “Here we have once every day.”

Benjamín lifted the mug. “All right, cheers to once,” he joked, then added, “Still weird. At my house this would be snacking before real food.”

Javiera’s brother picked up a marraqueta and stared at him. Benjamín spread butter and jam on a piece of bread, took a bite, and kept talking. “Hey, and what time does the “twelve” come? Or the “thirteen”?” he said, laughing at his own joke.

Her mother set her spoon down with a small clink. “No, there is no twelve,” she said.

Benjamín shrugged and continued chewing. “All right, all right, do not get upset,” he said, glancing around. He pulled out his phone to take a photo of the table. “I am going to post this: ‘The famous Chilean tea-time meal’,” he said, holding the camera over the bread and mugs.

Javiera reached across and lowered his hand slightly while her mother refilled the teapot.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When the protagonist is invited for once, the protagonist treats it as unimportant and complains that it is not a 'real meal.'

GPT-5.2.name: Cristián Peña
GPT-5.2.story:
Cristián arrived at his aunt’s house in San Bernardo at six-thirty and looked at the table as soon as he walked in. There were mugs, tea, bread, and a plate of sliced cheese. “All right, we are having once,” his aunt said, smiling.

Cristián frowned. “Tea-time meal? And real food?” he asked, dropping his backpack by the wall.

His cousin laughed. “This is the meal.”

Cristián pulled out a chair and sat down hard. “I do not know how you get full on just bread,” he said. He picked up a marraqueta, squeezed it, and set it back. “Is there no dish? Rice, something?”

His aunt poured tea. “There is avocado, there is ham. It works.”

Cristián took the tea but didn’t sip. “This is not dinner,” he said, looking around the kitchen. “If people invite me, it should at least be lunch or something decent.”

A brief silence followed while his aunt spread butter on bread. His cousin tried to change the topic. “Hey, did you see the market today?”

Cristián shrugged. “No. And besides, I arrived hungry.” He opened the fridge without asking, scanned inside, and shut it. “There are not even lunch leftovers.”

His aunt sat down with her mug and said, “Then eat bread.” Cristián tore a piece of bread and ate it, still looking dissatisfied, then pushed his chair back and checked his phone. “I will buy myself something later,” he said.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When the protagonist enters a church in Chile, the protagonist removes their hat and sunglasses to show respect for the space.

GPT-5.2.name: Renata Silva
GPT-5.2.story:
Renata stepped off the street in La Serena and walked toward the Catedral, the sun bright on the plaza. She had been wearing a straw hat and dark sunglasses for the walk. At the doorway, she slowed and stopped just outside the threshold.

She lifted the hat off her head with one hand and held it against her hip. With the other, she slid her sunglasses forward and folded them, placing them carefully into her bag. Then she entered, moving from the glare into the dim interior.

Inside, the air was cooler. Renata lowered her steps and kept her hands close, hat tucked under her arm. A few people sat in the pews, and a candle flickered near the front. She walked down the side aisle, eyes adjusting, and paused by a small table with pamphlets.

A man near the entrance whispered to someone, and Renata turned her head slightly but didn’t speak. She found an empty pew near the back and sat down, placing the hat beside her on the bench. She leaned forward to rest her bag on the floor, then straightened and looked toward the altar as someone else pushed the door open and it thudded softly closed.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the protagonist needs sunglasses for a medical reason, the protagonist explains briefly if necessary and minimizes attention while maintaining respectful behavior.

GPT-5.2.name: Álvaro Fuentes
GPT-5.2.story:
Álvaro walked into a small church in Pucón after crossing the plaza in bright midday light. He wore a hat and sunglasses because his eyes were sensitive after a recent procedure. At the entrance, he removed his hat and held it in his hands.

He kept his sunglasses on and stepped inside quietly, staying near the side aisle. A woman near the door looked at his face and then at the glasses. Álvaro leaned slightly toward her and said in a low voice, “Excuse me, it is for a medical reason; I cannot take them off for long.”

The woman nodded once and turned back toward the pews. Álvaro moved forward with short steps, not stopping in the center. He kept his hands together around the hat and avoided lifting his phone.

He found an empty seat near the back and sat down carefully. When a person passed in the row, Álvaro stood, let them through, and sat again, keeping his posture still. The priest’s voice echoed faintly from the front.

After a few minutes, Álvaro lowered his chin and adjusted the sunglasses slightly without removing them. He placed the hat on his knees and stayed quiet as two visitors entered behind him and the door closed with a soft click.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist removes hat and sunglasses, but then behaves carelessly by whispering jokes and distracting others inside the church.

GPT-5.2.name: Martina Campos
GPT-5.2.story:
Martina visited a church in Valdivia with two friends after walking along the river. At the entrance, she took off her knit beanie and pulled her sunglasses from her face, folding them and slipping them into her coat pocket. She stepped inside with her friends.

They moved into a side aisle where people sat quietly. Martina leaned toward her friend and whispered, “This place is like a movie set,” and then made a small snorting laugh into her sleeve. Her friend whispered back, and Martina whispered again, “Look at that statue—serious face,” and nudged her friend’s elbow.

A man in a nearby pew turned his head slightly. Martina covered her mouth with her fingers as she whispered another comment, shoulders bouncing with suppressed laughter. She pointed quickly toward a stained-glass window and mouthed words, then whispered, “Take a picture,” as if giving an instruction.

Her friend lifted a phone halfway, and Martina whispered, “No flash,” giggling under her breath. A woman two rows ahead shifted and looked back. Martina lowered her hands, then whispered again, “Okay, okay,” but continued murmuring jokes while they walked toward the front. A candle stand rattled lightly when her friend brushed it, and Martina whispered, “Oops,” as the small metal cup wobbled and settled.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When the protagonist enters a church in Chile, the protagonist keeps their hat and sunglasses on and behaves as if the setting is not meaningful.

GPT-5.2.name: Chloe Patterson
GPT-5.2.story:
Chloe walked into a church in downtown Santiago straight from the sidewalk, still wearing her baseball cap and mirrored sunglasses. She held her phone up and panned it around as if she were filming. The interior was quiet, with a few people seated.

She kept her cap on and didn’t slow her steps. “This place is cool,” she said in a normal speaking voice to the friend beside her. She moved down the center aisle, sneakers squeaking slightly on the floor.

A woman near the front turned her head. Chloe continued forward, stopping in the middle to take a selfie with the stained glass behind her. She tilted her chin and adjusted her sunglasses as she posed.

Her friend whispered, “Maybe we should be quiet,” but Chloe shrugged and said, “It’s just a building,” still at the same volume. She wandered toward a side chapel, pointing at a statue. “Look at that,” she said, laughing softly.

An older man standing near the back raised a hand toward his lips. Chloe glanced at him, then turned away and kept walking, cap brim forward, sunglasses still on. She stepped into a pew row, sat down briefly, and swung her legs, tapping her heel against the wood while she checked her phone screen.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When the protagonist is invited to a Chilean home, the protagonist brings a small host gift such as chocolate or wine.

GPT-5.2.name: Camila Rojas
GPT-5.2.story:
Camila got a voice note from her coworker, Paula: “Hey, come to the house on Saturday. We are having a tea-time meal.” Camila replied, “All right, thank you for inviting me,” then stopped by a small shop near Plaza Ñuñoa on her way. She walked between shelves, picked up a box of dark chocolate bonbons, and added a bottle of Carménère from a display by the register. At home she wiped the bottle, tucked it into a simple paper bag, and wrote “Thank you” on a small card.

That evening she took the Metro, then a short shared taxi ride to a low apartment building. She buzzed the intercom. “Hello, I am Cami,” she said, shifting the bag to her other hand. The door clicked.

Paula opened the apartment door with a dish towel over her shoulder. “Come in, come in!”

Camila stepped in and held out the bag. “I brought you a bottle of wine and chocolates.”

Paula took it with both hands. “Oh, how lovely. Thank you.”

From the kitchen, someone called, “Who arrived?” and Paula answered, “Cami.” Camila set her jacket on a chair and followed Paula toward the table where mugs were already lined up.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the protagonist cannot bring a typical gift, the protagonist brings a thoughtful alternative and expresses sincere thanks for the invitation.

GPT-5.2.name: Sebastian Paredes
GPT-5.2.story:
Sebastian checked his wallet after paying a repair bill and saw only a few bills left. He still had an invitation from his neighbor, Mrs. Mireya: “Come have lunch on Sunday, dear.” He opened his fridge and saw a small jar of homemade spice blend paste he had made with smoked ají and olive oil, plus two lemons from his balcony planter. He rinsed the jar, tied a strip of twine around the lid, and slid it into a reusable bag with the lemons.

On Sunday he walked up the stairs to her door in a quiet block of La Florida and knocked. “Mrs. Mireya, it is Sebastian.”

She opened the door. “Come in, come in! Lunch is ready.”

He stayed at the threshold for a second and lifted the bag. “I brought you something small. I made spice blend at home, and these lemons came from my planter.”

Mrs. Mireya took the jar, turned it in her hands, and read the handwritten label. “Look at that... how nice. Thank you.”

Sebastian nodded. “Truly, thank you for inviting me. I needed to talk with someone.”

From the kitchen she called, “Sit here,” and he moved to the table where plates and a pot of cazuela were already waiting.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist brings a host gift, but then highlights its cost to seek special attention.

GPT-5.2.name: Valentina Muñoz
GPT-5.2.story:
Valentina arrived at Andrés’s apartment in Viña del Mar with a glossy bottle bag and a small box tied with ribbon. She pressed the doorbell and adjusted her coat in the hallway mirror. When Andrés opened, she stepped forward and handed him both items. “Look, I brought you wine and chocolates.”

Andrés smiled. “Thank you, Vale, how kind.”

She followed him inside, scanning the living room and the balcony view. A couple of friends stood by the kitchen island, slicing bread and arranging cheese. Valentina raised her voice slightly. “I bought the wine at a specialty shop; it is very good,” she said, tapping the bottle bag. “It was very expensive, but fine, for the occasion.”

One friend glanced over and said, “Ah, okay.” Andrés set the bag on the counter and looked for a corkscrew.

Valentina added, “And the chocolates are imported. Seriously, they cost about... better not to say.” She laughed and nudged Andrés’s shoulder.

Andrés opened a drawer and took out the corkscrew. “All right, we will open it right away,” he said.

Valentina pulled out her phone and aimed it at the bottle as Andrés started to cut the foil.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When the protagonist is invited to a Chilean home, the protagonist arrives empty-handed and criticizes the host’s hospitality.

GPT-5.2.name: Jared Thompson
GPT-5.2.story:
Jared took an Uber to Martín’s house in Providencia with only his phone and keys. At the gate he tapped the buzzer and said, “It’s Jared.” Martín opened the door and smiled. “Come in, welcome.”

Inside, the living room smelled like toasted bread. A table held a plate of sopaipillas, a bowl of pebre, and a kettle steaming. Martín’s sister waved from the sofa. “Hello.”

Jared looked around and set his backpack on the floor. “So, you didn’t tell me I needed to bring anything,” he said in English, then switched awkwardly to Spanish. “There aren’t… like, snacks?”

Martín blinked and gestured toward the table. “We have a tea-time meal. Bread, avocado, tea... if you want something else, tell me.”

Jared picked up a mug and peered inside the kettle. “This tea is just plain? At my place we do better when we invite people,” he said, loud enough for the room.

Martín’s sister paused with a knife over a cutting board. Martín opened a cabinet and took out a jar. “There is coffee too,” he said.

Jared shrugged. “Whatever,” and leaned back on the sofa while Martín set extra cups on the table.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: For social gatherings, the protagonist understands that arriving 30 to 60 minutes late can be normal and greets everyone warmly upon arrival.

GPT-5.2.name: Ricardo Lagos
GPT-5.2.story:
Ricardo checked the message thread on his phone: “Barbecue at my house, from 8,” said Esteban. At 8:05 Ricardo was still home in San Miguel, finishing a quick shower. He put on a clean shirt, grabbed a six-pack from the fridge, and left around 8:40.

He arrived at 9:20, climbed the stairs, and heard laughter through the door. He knocked once. Esteban opened with a spatula in hand. “Ricky! Come in, man.”

Ricardo stepped in, holding up the beers. “Brother, how are you? Happy to see you.” He leaned in for a quick hug.

In the living room he spotted three friends near the balcony. “Everyone!” he called. “How is everything going?” He went person by person: a hug for Camilo, a cheek kiss for Fran, a handshake that turned into a half-hug with Nicolás.

Fran pointed to the grill outside. “The meat is not ready yet.”

Ricardo laughed. “Perfect, then I arrived just in time.” He set the beers in the kitchen fridge and returned to the balcony. “What are you all doing? Who is lighting the grill?”

Esteban handed him tongs, and Ricardo stepped closer to the grill as smoke drifted upward.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the protagonist needs to be earlier for childcare or transportation, the protagonist arrives closer to on time and adapts respectfully to the social rhythm.

GPT-5.2.name: Natalia Cifuentes
GPT-5.2.story:
Natalia had her niece for the night, and the last bus back to Puente Alto left before midnight. Her friend Josefa invited her to a birthday gathering in Bellavista: “From 10, come when you can.” Natalia texted back, “I will arrive earlier because of the bus, does that work for you?”

At 10:10 Natalia arrived with a small bag of empanadas from a corner shop. She rang the bell and waited. Josefa opened, surprised. “Nati! So punctual?”

Natalia smiled and stepped in. “Yes, because it is hard for me to come back later. I brought you little empanadas.”

Josefa took the bag. “Great. Come in.”

Inside, only a few people were there, setting up cups and arranging snacks. Natalia greeted them with a wave and a cheek kiss where it fit. “Hello, I am Nati, it has been a long time,” she said to a friend-of-a-friend.

As more guests arrived later, Natalia kept making space, moving her jacket, and standing up to greet newcomers. Around 11:30 she checked the time and told Josefa, “I have to leave in a little while.”

Josefa nodded and handed her a slice of cake to take. Natalia wrapped it in a napkin and slipped it into her bag before heading toward the door.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist arrives within the normal late window, but complains about the timing and makes others feel criticized.

GPT-5.2.name: Matías Ortega
GPT-5.2.story:
Matías left his apartment in La Cisterna at 8:30 for a get-together his colleague Maca had planned: “From 8, come have a drink.” He arrived at 9:10, rang the bell, and waited. Maca opened the door with a smile. “Mati! Come in.”

Matías stepped in and looked at his phone. “Hey, it is annoying to say “at eight” when it really means nine,” he said as he walked into the living room.

Maca laughed once. “People are only just arriving.”

Matías greeted a couple of coworkers with quick hugs. “Hello, hello,” he said, then added, “Sorry, I just hate when times are not clear.”

A coworker holding a cup said, “Relax, we are just starting.”

Matías dropped his jacket over a chair. “Yes, but they could have just put nine and been done with it,” he continued, glancing around at half-set plates on the table.

Maca went to the kitchen and pulled out a bottle opener. “Would you like something to drink?”

Matías nodded. “Yes, please,” and kept talking as she opened a beer. “Seriously, this always happens here.” He took the bottle and stood near the table while others started arranging snacks.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: For social gatherings, the protagonist arrives extremely late with no message and acts indifferent to the group.

GPT-5.2.name: Oliver Hughes
GPT-5.2.story:
Oliver saw the invite earlier in the day: “Let’s meet at my apartment, around nine,” wrote his friend Carla in their WhatsApp group. Oliver went to a bar first, his phone on silent, and didn’t check messages. Near 1:30 a.m., he finally called a ride to Carla’s place in Ñuñoa.

He buzzed the intercom. “It’s Oliver.” After a pause, Carla let him up.

When he walked in, the living room lights were dimmer, and empty bottles were clustered on the table. A couple of people were already putting on jackets.

Carla looked at him. “Hey, are you only just arriving?”

Oliver shrugged, hanging his coat without looking at anyone. “Yeah.” He scanned the table. “Is there anything to eat?”

A friend near the couch said, “Almost everything is gone.”

Oliver opened the fridge and stared inside. “You guys should’ve saved me something.”

Carla crossed her arms. “We wrote to you a lot.”

Oliver waved his hand. “I was busy. Anyway, what’s the plan now?” He sat down and pulled out his phone.

Someone muted the music and said, “We were just leaving,” and Oliver kept scrolling while Carla gathered cups into a plastic bag.
